author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Improve job execution predictor efficiency (#442)

* frontend: Optimize ExecutionPredictor and add test coverage

Replace the single-unit stepping in predictNextExecution with direct jumps
to the next valid hour/minute and precompute wildcard flags plus membership
sets once per schedule (reused across predictNextExecutions), preserving the
exact behavior of the original algorithm.

Add ExecutionPredictor.test.js with unit tests, a randomized equivalence
check against the original implementation, and runtime benchmarks.

* api: Optimize ExecutionPredictor runtime

Precompute wildcard flags and membership lookup tables in the constructor,
and jump directly to the next valid hour/minute instead of stepping one
unit at a time, mirroring the frontend optimization while preserving the
exact behavior of the original algorithm.

Declare the previously dynamic expiresAt property for PHP 8.2 compatibility.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message: docker: Fix dev mode, add dev.sh script

author: santichausis
message:
frontend: Parse crontab lines to populate schedule and request fields (#441)

When a user pastes a full crontab line such as
"*/5 * * * * curl https://example.com/api -X POST", the system now
detects the cron schedule prefix, extracts it, and populates both
the execution schedule and the URL/method/headers/body/auth fields.

Detection works in both the URL field (inline banner) and the
"Import from cURL" dialog, which now also accepts wget commands
and crontab lines.

Closes #12.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
docker: Add development mode with live-mounted sources

Add a docker-compose.dev.yml override that runs the frontend and status
page via the React dev server (npm start) with the source live-mounted,
so changes are visible immediately with hot reloading. The PHP API source
is live-mounted as well. The www reverse proxy is pointed at the dev
servers on :3000 and forwards the HMR WebSocket upgrade.

frontend, api: Extend dashboard (#436)

* Minor improvements

author: santichausis
message:
frontend: Explain job failure modes and HTTP error codes in history (#429)

* frontend: Explain job failure modes and HTTP error codes in history

Closes #250.

The execution-details dialog showed the raw status only ("404 Not
Found", "Failed (timeout)", ...) with no hint of what it means or how to
fix it. It now renders a short, plain-language explanation under the
status whenever an execution failed.

- New frontend/src/utils/JobStatusInfo.js maps a result to an i18n token:
    * the non-HTTP failure modes (DNS, connect, timeout, size, URL,
      internal, others) each get a dedicated explanation;
    * HTTP errors map to a dedicated message for the common codes
      (400/401/403/404/405/429/500/502/503/504) and fall back to a
      per-class message for anything else (3xx/4xx/5xx).
  The mapping returns null for successful or unknown executions, so the
  note only appears when there is something useful to say.

- HistoryDetails renders the explanation as an info Alert below the
  status row.

- English and German phrases for all explanations.

Tests:
  * frontend/src/utils/JobStatusInfo.test.js -- 10 unit tests over the
    code/class fallback logic.
  * frontend/src/components/jobs/HistoryDetails.test.jsx -- 4 React
    Testing Library tests rendering the dialog (against the real English
    phrases) for a 404, an uncommon 5xx, a timeout, and a successful
    run that shows no note.

* frontend: Show status explanations in test run and job list too

Follow-up to the review on #429: reuse the failure-mode / HTTP-error
explanations in two more places.

- JobTestRun: render the same explanation below the result status. It is
  suppressed when the dedicated redirect note is already shown, so a 3xx
  isn't explained twice.

- Job list: wrap the last-execution status in a tooltip carrying the
  explanation. The list only has the status (not the HTTP code), so
  statusExplanationToken() now falls back to a generic "modes.FAILED_HTTPERROR"
  message that points the user to the execution details for the exact code.

- New en/de phrase for that generic HTTP-error fallback; JobStatusInfo
  tests updated for the new fallback behaviour.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Add simple incidents feature for status pages (#430)

* api, frontend, statuspage: Add manual incidents for status pages.

Let users create and manage incidents in the console and display active and recent resolved incidents on public status pages.

Closes #267

* docker: Add statuspage support

* statuspage: Make incident description optional.

Allow empty descriptions in the API and console, and keep past incident rows visually consistent when there is nothing to expand.

* statuspage: Add incident phrases to all locales.

Complete translations for active/past incident headings and start-date formatting in the 13 locales that were still missing them.

* Use integer status values for status page incidents.

Represent incident status as 0/1 with named constants in the API and console instead of booleans, leaving room for future enum values.

* Add incident closed_at with console editing and public display.

Store an optional end timestamp for resolved incidents, auto-fill it when marking resolved, and show end dates on public past incidents with start dates in the expanded view.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Fix scheduler lag dashboard and alert for master-only nodes.

Exclude non-executor instances from lag queries, document negative lag semantics, and alert when lag exceeds -5s instead of the unreachable >1s threshold.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
chronos: Add prometheus metrics, update cctz, timezone handling fixes (#426)

* design: Add prometheus-metrics.md

* design: Refine chronos Prometheus metrics spec to match runtime behavior.

Align metric definitions with how scheduling, workers, and persistence actually work, and add config, alerting, and implementation guidance.

* design: Update prometheus metrics design doc

* chronos: Implement Prometheus metrics exposition and instrumentation.

Add prometheus-cpp metrics on :9098 with batched worker/scheduler hot paths, RPC event-handler instrumentation, and resilient UpdateThread MySQL error handling.

* Add Grafana chronos dashboard and metrics guidance for agents.

Document hot-path batching, RpcThrow, MetricsLabels, and related conventions in AGENTS.md alongside an importable operational dashboard.

* Add chronos_worker_threads gauge for active worker thread count.

Track live worker threads per job_type with atomic inc/dec in WorkerThread, and extend the Grafana dashboard to show active vs started threads.

* Add chronos_worker_thread_lifetime_seconds histogram.

Record wall-clock worker thread duration once per threadMain exit and expose p50/p95 on the Grafana dashboard.

* Add Prometheus alert rules for chronos metrics.

Define recording rules and 22 alerts for scheduler, execution, persistence, notifications, and RPC, with an 80% job success rate threshold aligned to the Grafana dashboard.

* Tune chronos alert thresholds and drop auto-disable alert.

Raise worker jitter warning to 45s and remove ChronosJobsAutoDisabled since job auto-disable is expected behavior.

* Raise chronos network failure alert thresholds to 10%.

DNS, connect, and timeout failure spike alerts were too noisy at 5% given normal job failure rates.

* chronos: Fix spiky update thread backlog metric

* chronos: Apply same fix to other threads

* chronos: Update cctz to v2.5 for modern tzdata compatibility.

Older cctz failed to load zones like Africa/Casablanca on recent
IANA tzdata, causing enabled jobs in those timezones to be skipped.

* chronos: Skip empty timezones

author: santichausis
message:
frontend: Auto-detect curl/wget commands pasted into the URL field (#423)

Closes #103.

Users frequently paste a whole `curl`/`wget` command line into the job
URL field (often including a trailing `> /dev/null`). Instead of saving
that as a broken URL, the editor now detects it and offers a one-click
import that fills in the URL, request method, headers, body and basic
auth -- the same job model the "Import from cURL" dialog (#421) already
populates.

Implementation:

- New frontend/src/utils/CommandParser.js, built on top of the existing
  CurlParser tokenizer:
    * looksLikeHttpCommand() -- a cheap detector the URL field calls on
      change. It matches a leading curl/wget command word (tolerating a
      prompt, sudo, env assignments and an executable path) but not a
      normal URL such as https://curl.example.com.
    * parseWget() -- wget has its own flag semantics (e.g. -O takes a
      value, --post-data implies POST, --header/--method/--user/
      --password/--user-agent/--referer), so it gets a dedicated parser
      rather than reusing curl's flag tables. The classic monitoring
      form `wget -q -O /dev/null URL` parses correctly.
    * parseHttpCommand() -- dispatches to the curl or wget parser.

- JobEditor wiring: when the pasted text parses to a command with a URL,
  an inline info banner appears under the URL field with an Import
  action. Importing reuses the existing apply logic (renamed
  applyImportedCurl -> applyImportedCommand since it now serves curl,
  wget and the dialog).

- Programmatic URL updates now remount the URL field (via a key bump) so
  the displayed value reflects the cleaned URL after an import or a
  test-run redirect; ValidatingTextField keeps its value in internal
  state and did not otherwise pick up the change.

- English and German phrases for the new banner.

Tests: frontend/src/utils/CommandParser.test.js adds 26 unit tests
covering the detector, the wget parser and the dispatcher; the existing
CurlParser and CurlImportDialog suites continue to pass (84 total).

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Fix malformed JobNotificationSettings table in REST API docs.

Widen grid table columns so onSslCertExpirySeconds and the default value fit within column boundaries.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Encode non-ASCII notification mail header values as RFC 2047 words.

Subjects and display names with UTF-8 characters were sent raw, which breaks MIME parsing; Q-encode them as =?UTF-8?Q?...?= instead.

author: Patrick Schlangen
message:
Capture SSL certificate expiry for each request, add expiry notification (#424)

* chronos: Capture SSL certificate expiry in JobResult

Store the leaf certificate's expiry as a Unix timestamp on HTTPS job
results via libcurl CERTINFO, with parsing for common OpenSSL and ISO
date formats.

* chronos: Persist SSL certificate expiry in joblog_ssl table

Add schema version 3 with a joblog_ssl satellite table keyed by
joblogid, and store sslCertExpiry from HTTPS job results when available.

* chronos: Expose SSL certificate expiry in test run status

Add sslCertExpiry to TestRunStatus in the Thrift protocol and wire it
through TestRunThread into getJobTestRunStatus.

* chronos: Expose SSL certificate expiry in job log entries

Add optional sslCertExpiry to JobLogEntry and read it from joblog_ssl
when returning job history via getJobLog and getJobLogDetails.

* chronos: Store SSL certificate expiry on the job table

Add ssl_cert_expiry to the node job schema, persist it from job results,
and expose it as sslCertExpiryDate on JobExecutionInfo.

* api: Regenerate PHP thrift stubs for SSL cert expiry fields

Rename JobExecutionInfo.sslCertExpiry to match other structs, regenerate
the PHP protocol glue, and remove obsolete stub files from old RPC names.

* api: Expose sslCertExpiry in Job, History, and REST API docs

Surface TLS certificate expiry from Thrift in API responses and document
the field on Job and HistoryItem in the REST API reference.

* chronos: Join joblog_ssl only on SQLite schema v3 or newer

Avoid history read failures on daily log databases that have not yet
been migrated by checking PRAGMA user_version before joining joblog_ssl.

* Add SSL certificate expiry notification settings to job schema

Introduce notify_ssl_cert_expiry and notify_ssl_cert_expiry_seconds on the
node job table and wire them through Thrift, chronos, and the PHP API.

* docs: Document SSL certificate expiry notification settings in REST API

Add onSslCertExpiry and onSslCertExpirySeconds to JobNotificationSettings
and the job details response example.

* chronos: Send SSL certificate expiry notifications once per cert

Queue SSL_CERT_EXPIRY notifications when a job enters its warning window,
deduplicate via ssl_cert_expiry_notified on the job table, and extend
Thrift and mail handling for the new notification type.

* chronos: Fix userID regression in scheduled job execution

Pass row[10] (userid) to HTTPRequest::fromURL instead of row[11]
(request_method), which caused GET jobs to run with userID 0.

* frontend: Add SSL expiry notifications and fix dashboard links

Expose SSL cert expiry notification settings in the job editor, add
notification type labels, and fix dashboard history links for
notification events by resolving job folder IDs.

* frontend: Always show SSL expiry lead-time input in job editor

Match the failure notification UI by keeping the days field visible
even when the SSL expiry notification toggle is off.

* frontend: Fix german grammar error

* frontend: Show SSL cert expiry in test run results

Display a lock icon with tooltip next to the peer address when the
test run returns a TLS certificate expiry date.

* frontend: Show SSL cert expiry in history details

Extract SslCertExpiryIcon for reuse in test run and history detail
views, and move the tooltip string to jobs.sslCertExpiry.
